item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My absolute favorite SCP for the last couple of years of my reading the site, and I still come back to it regularly. I just wanted to tip my hat at your highly effective characterization and worldbuilding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't really get a lot of the stuff here before reading ''The Worm'', but now I admit this skip is really neat. Also take this +1.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The first image is public domain.

The second image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

The last image was sourced back to here, without an explicit Creative Commons license.

Last two images have since been removed.

item.title: Stairs leading to walls
item.content:encoded: and other references to nonsensical architecture reminded me of the Winchester Mystery House. Any inspiration there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip always felt unnerving by how ominous the clinical tone makes this skip sound. Grand writing style.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of a Nit-pick but I hate the Photo used for the 'monster'. It feels really cheesy and something out of a halloween prop store.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone else see hints of Werewolf The Apocalypse here? Because I all kinds of do, and I am not displeased.

item.title: Currently slapping myself in the face
item.content:encoded:
Oh my goodness! I suppose I was given all the right clues, and just couldn't piece them together, now that I know that, it all actually makes sense…

Ach, I was probably paying more attention to the Broken God himself as opposed to any of his individual parts…

item.title: Re: Is it REALLY Broken God?
item.content:encoded:
• There's a 12 cubic meter space missing from the machine in the last sublevel.
  • Said machine was apparently the heart of this thing.
  • From the tale, said machine made people want to feed it, at least one of them willingly merging with it.
  • Also from the tale, the workers could hear it moving, despite its creator having turned it off.
  • The machine was apparently a representation of an all-consuming entity that wanted to bring itself into existance.

This is SCP-882's origin story, and it fits pretty well with how the Church was characterized before it was retconned into being sorta good.

Admittedly, none of it is in the expunged sections.

item.title: Is it REALLY Broken God?
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I see that last line, and yeah, the author keeps saying he left clues.
[DATA EXPUNGED] really doesn't TELL me anything, though. I'm really not seeing the 'clues' that are supposedly 'littered' throughout this article.
-1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In retrospect, this makes a ton of sense re:Sarkics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<morbo>THE PERIODIC TABLE DOES NOT WORK THAT WAY</morbo>

(oh, I've already downvoted this one, how annoying)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this piece as soon as I read the Task Force name. Shout outs to Task Force - WTF!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Besides the Broken God reference which is obvious, there are four in total. Glad you liked it, Wishun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The sandstone itself isn't anomalous, just extraterrestrial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this a bit more with every draft and revision I saw, and I like the finished product most of all.

Do you have a final count of the easter eggs? I noticed 3 nods to other scps, but I'm cheating with insider knowledge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Argh I want to downvote this for the poor tone, the weird formatting, the [DATA EXPUNGED] for an entire room, and for it not really making any sense unless you read The Worm…

… but god damn once you do read The Worm and then re-read this, it's bloody compelling.

+1, but I didn't want to

it is, in fact, very much like Stephen King. Great ideas, great story, great characters, but poor execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just me, but I prefer to see the Broken God as it is shown in the Mary and Salah tales. Namely, that it's genuinely benevolent. But that's Headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The sandstone is an issue. If it's constituent bits are all anomolous, that needs to be spelled out.

"Severe" is jarring. That really needs another word.

Otherwise, it's neat. It's honestly hurt by the existence of the awesome tale that precedes it, but taken on it's own it's a fair +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are two different groups at work in this scip and I wanted to differentiate. The "cult" refers to the group of people who were actively helping to build the scip, and were either present when it disappeared or entered it when it reappeared to loot it and then escape.

The Church of the Broken God is either what became of that cult, or the cult was absorbed into the Church. I won't say which.

I realize sandstone isn't an element but I don't know enough about geology to list everything through. For the sake of brevity let's just assume that all of the elements in the gray sandstone are unknown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which I would put forward to suggest it's more of a placebo addition, to give those who are exposing themselves to anomalous forces some psychological reinforcement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really dislike the NBCA thing, because the generic labeling implies that there's a sort of reliable, one-size-fits-most shielding method from anomalous effects, as opposed to the suits being shielded against a more specific type of anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a bit on the fence myself, but leaning toward "meh". There's some interesting stuff and some not so interesting stuff. For instance, I like the "expanded periodic table" concept (and the NBCA— both suggest a Foundation a little more well-versed in the weirdness of the world in which they exist), but sandstone isn't an element, it's a stone comprised of numerous elements. Even if this stuff was just a single element, calling it by that name suggests numerous things you may not have wished to suggest.

Also, you definitely shouldn't refer to the Church in-text as "the cult".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ultimately the concept behind the scip itself is the nature of the Broken God. Is this scip the origin of the Broken God? IS it the Broken God? Is the Worm the Broken God? Is the Man the Broken God? Which came first? What IS the Broken God, besides being broken? Is the Worm something the man trapped and was trapped by, or did he create the whole thing in a fight of existential madness? Maybe his insanity lured something primordial to him and he caught it, but in doing so transformed it, tarnishing its purity or simply giving a shape to its wrongness. Or maybe he found a piece of the Broken God, and created a mythology of his own around it, one that his madness helped shape?

There are many clues in this entry. I don't want to come out and say it, because 1) there is no canon and 2) I want people to build their own conclusions as to what it is. I want to reward astute readers like yourself who recognize the clues and look deeper into other parts of the site. Once they see more of the whole picture, they might come up with inspirations of their own, upvote other scips, and generally build on the mythology of the site.

Plus, it was mostly Wishun's idea, which admittedly was a really neat idea and I felt it needed writing down. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote for now.

At first, I really liked this skip.

Then I read the tale.

I REALLY loved the tale and upvoted it. But there is really nothing in this skip that's not also in the tale. One is a clinical article that's a small window of what we see in the tale. The tale tells us what's really going on. And believe me, I think its its a great, wonderful idea but I'm not giving you multiple upvotes for the same idea. This needs something more, a bit more of a hook, something new that the Foundation knows about it that we didn't already get from "The Worm."

EDIT: Also, you tried to tie this to SCP-882, didn't you? This bit here: " The assembly has suffered extensive damage, with evidence that a large section (approximately 12 cubic meters) has been violently removed. " That's the same size as SCP-882 (post recovery).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a "steampunk Frankenstein's monster" someone created for an art class. I originally intended to just show an old Victorian-era prosthetic hand but I realized it was a little too dated and probably already in use somewhere on-site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second image is way, way, way too clear and it makes it obvious it's a model. It's almost a promotional shot. (Heck, it probably WAS, wherever you got it from.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh. Well it's a clue. This one is a little blunt, but it's meant to remind you that this is related to the CotBG. The clues are in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that's interesting, because you're reading verbatim some vital clues as to this scip's relation to the Church of the Broken God. If I changed them, there's a chance people won't detect the clues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will say this. The Church of the Broken God is involved with this scip. The clues are there, if you dig in the right places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The origins of the tale and scip were sparked by my disappointment in A:MfP, and a desire to see something more cosmic-horror like the first game, so you're not far off on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good, thorough review. I'll likely take them to heart and make the necessary cleanups ASAP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my mind's eye I saw an exposed spinal column with metal plates bolted to it like scales. I guess I translated it kind of wonky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's a god without a little damnation to hold over your followers' heads?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't decide whether or not this is a great improvement to the original tale or if I preferred The Worm as a single article. Still, by itself, it is a great SCP. A bit of an A Machine For Pigs vibe, a slight Resident Evil/House of the Dead slant at the beginning, but it all seems to be coincidental. I can't tell whether or not this is actually related to the Church or if, like Dr. Nietzsche suggested, the Foundation misinterpreting it, but either way, I really like Deus Est Machina cults.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I'm on the fence. It's interesting and I think I like the "House what makes Frankenstein's Monsters," and the CotBG reference works for me, but doesn't really jump out at me, and it has "Alice, Bob, and [DATA EXPUNGED] going on with both

constructed from brass, iron, copper, gold, bamboo, carved jade and [DATA EXPUNGED]

and

Orb Room (Sub-Level Ten): [DATA EXPUNGED]

which is a pet peeve of mine. No vote for now due to the expungements. Clean that up and you'd have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me likey

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This gives such a strong "Amnesia: a machine for pigs" vibe that I'm gonna squeal.

It also needs more meat and a better hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's the beautiful part. It isn't. Or rather, I don't see it as that. The Foundation, in their bid to make sense of a universe that has for a aeons ceased to make sense, throws this in with the Broken God. Because it says it right down there at the bottom, doesn't it?! It has to be part of them! Who else but us, the GoC, the Insurgents, a few dicks(AWCY?) and they themselves know of the Broken God?

But this isn't something built from parts of a scattered God, to be reunited and made whole. It's a Hell made of iron, oil, brass, bone and blood, crafted and carved with human hands. The God-Once-Broken was nowhere near this, no god ever was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Click to Show Full Review
Click to Hide

The facility is constructed primarily from concrete, iron, and brass; but also a number of exotic and/or unknown material

Semicolons do not work that way; you should look to the oatmeal comic about them.

severe reinforcement to the spinal column

This is really awkward.

and hostile entities within
All instances of SCP-1461-1 are considered extremely hostile

I feel that at some point, we get it. These super-augmented things don't like us.

It is theorized that SCP-1461 itself may "command" SCP-1461-1

I feel that the quotations here are unnecessary. It could be replaced with "…may have influence over the behavior of SCP-XXXX." Or some shit like that.

personnel suggests some kind of guiding, hostile intelligence.

More hostility. I feel at this point I get the picture, it hates the Foundation, can we stop piling on the evidence of 'look how much it hates the foundation IT HATES US THIIIIS MUUUCH'.

repeating stream of speech peppered with religious symbolism

"peppered" is odd tone.

Orb Room (Sub-Level Ten): [DATA EXPUNGED]

I don't agree with this expungement. I find it sloppy.


Overall, nice skip, a few things are bothersome, not enough for me to upvote. Or maybe I'm just tired and therefore too grumpy to enjoy it thoroughly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
severe reinforcement to the spinal column

You what now?

Other than that line, I enjoyed this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lol Goo goo ga joob.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not exemplary, but I liked it enough to upvote. Also, I'm just warning you that if you continue to write Worm stuff I'm eventually going to do a full version of that thing I posted on the discussion of the original tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's a thing what makes frankensteins, with what seems like a forced CotBG reference? Meh. No vote.

Edit: this is better than I had originally thought, so +1, but I still hate that last line you wedged in
